texto: For great changes in the human mind are terrible. As we realize them we realize the limitless possibilities of sinister deeds that lie hidden in every human being. A little child that loves a doll can become an old, crafty, secret murderer. How horrible! And perhaps it is still more horrible to think that, while the human envelope remains totally unchanged, every word of the letter within may become altered, and a message of peace fade into a sentence of death.
explicacao: Citação de Robert S. Hichens sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “For great changes in the human mind are terrible. As we realize them we realize the limitless pos…” articula reflexao, evil, humanity em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como luto e mudanca.

texto: It was a black and white day of frost, which crawled along the dark trees and outlined twig and branch. The air was misty, and distant objects assumed a mysterious importance. Slight sounds, too, suggested infinite activities to the mind.("A Tribute Of Souls")
explicacao: Citação de Robert S. Hichens sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “It was a black and white day of frost, which crawled along the dark trees and outlined twig and b…” articula reflexao, frost, winter em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e winter.

texto: Some say that it is lack of imagination which makes men and women brutes. May it not be power of imagination? The interest of torturing is lessened, is almost lost, if we can not be the tortured as well as the torturer.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Robert S. Hichens que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Some say that it is lack of imagination which makes men and women brutes. May it not be power of …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, imagination, torture, com foco em torture.
